element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary traversal mechanic throughout first half

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy jumping (defeating by landing on foes)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Replenishable meter mechanic, used alongside jumping

element_introduction_order.element: Environmental hazards (water, lava, rolling boulders, stalactites)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Instant death mechanics driving precision platforming
Optional content providing extra lives and continues

element_introduction_order.element: Timon's bug-hunting stage (time limit, spider avoidance)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Prevents excessive life loss from single mistakes
Game Over recovery mechanism, collectable in levels

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Level 2: Can't Wait to be King
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Late-development increase in complexity to meet playtime targets

typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of instant-death hazards (water, lava, boulders, stalactites)
Spike in difficulty (monkey puzzle expansion in Can't Wait to be King)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating between safe exploration/collection phases and hazard-intensive platforming sections; bonus minigames provide structural relief before returning to main challenge

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden bugs trigger bonus stages; secret passageways reward wall exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Wall-jumpable surfaces in Be Prepared hide continue and items behind visible barriers

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic jumping and roar mechanics in safe context; cub form encourages non-lethal approach via jumping on enemies
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Environmental hazards introduced (water, lava, boulders, stalactites); monkey puzzle in Can't Wait to be King deliberately expanded late in development to increase challenge; bug collection becomes riskier with spider presence
difficulty_curve.late_game: Transition to adult Simba with combat moves; sustained exposure to instant-death mechanics; sustained pressure from advanced platforming sections
notable_spikes: Can't Wait to be King (monkey puzzle expanded to meet Disney playtime criteria)
Introduction of environmental instant-death hazards (mid-game shift from enemy-based threat to environmental threat)
The Stampede (music track indicates high-intensity action, though track cuts short in final version)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels follow narrative of 1994 film; two notable levels (Hakuna Matata and Be Prepared) were adapted from scenes cut from the final movie
First half of game features Simba as cub; primary mechanic is jumping on enemies; roar stun ability complements jumping-based approach
Second half of game features Simba as adult with expanded combat (scratching, mauling, throws) replacing jumping-on-enemies
Mid-to-late game sections introduce instant-death mechanics (water, lava, rolling boulders, falling stalactites) as primary tension source replacing enemy-focused threat
Hidden bugs trigger optional Timon and Pumbaa stages that break main platforming rhythm; Timon uses time pressure, Pumbaa uses precision timing; provide resource rewards (lives, continues)
Hidden passageways and wall-accessible areas contain continues and items; Be Prepared features wall-jumpable path with hidden continue behind visual barrier

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Lion King SNES article)
wikipedia (The Lion King video game article)
